name: Introduction
statement_text:
For a general linear method (A, U, B, V ), the ‘stability
matrix’ M (z) is defined by

                         M (z) = V + zB(I − zA)−1 U.

As we have anticipated, we have the following result:
statement_latex:
\begin{definition}
For a general linear method $(A, U, B, V)$, the `stability matrix' $M(z)$ is defined by
\[
M(z) = V + zB(I - zA)^{-1}U.
\]
As we have anticipated, we have the following result:
\end{definition}
context_latex:
\paragraph{Context}
The definition concerns the stability matrix \(M(z)\) for a general linear method \((A, U, B, V)\) applied to the linear test equation
\[
y'(x) = qy(x) \quad (520a),
\]
where \(z = hq\). The method transforms an incoming vector \(y^{[n-1]}\) to an outgoing vector \(y^{[n]}\) via \(M(z)\):
\[
y^{[n]} = M(z)y^{[n-1]} \quad (520b).
\]
Variables: \(A, U, B, V\) are the coefficient matrices of the method; \(z = hq\) with stepsize \(h\); \(M(z)\) is an \(r \times r\) matrix function.

variables.name: A
variables.definition: A is the coefficient matrix in the general linear method (A, U, B, V)

variables.name: U
variables.definition: U is the coefficient matrix in the general linear method (A, U, B, V)

variables.name: B
variables.definition: B is the coefficient matrix in the general linear method (A, U, B, V)

variables.name: V
variables.definition: V is the coefficient matrix in the general linear method (A, U, B, V)

variables.name: z
variables.definition: z = hq, where h is the stepsize and q is the parameter in the test equation y'(x) = qy(x)

variables.name: M(z)
variables.definition: M(z) is an r × r matrix-valued function of z that relates the incoming vector y^{[n-1]} to the outgoing vector y^{[n]} via y^{[n]} = M(z)y^{[n-1]}

preamble: The definition concerns the stability matrix M(z) for a general linear method (A, U, B, V) applied to the linear test equation y' = qy, where z = hq. The method transforms an incoming vector y^{[n-1]} to an outgoing vector y^{[n]} via M(z).

dependencies.name: stability in the sense of Dahlquist
